summary: Comprehensive backend testing of Syncra AI completed. All 21 pytest tests passed (100%) covering health check, Google OAuth URL builder, OAuth callback redirect handling, auth guards on protected endpoints, fake-session-based authenticated flow (me/tasks/stats/preferences), full task CRUD with completion + stats reflection, preferences defaults+update, logout session invalidation, and Gmail/AI module import sanity. Email/AI live calls intentionally skipped per request (require real Google consent).

minor.issue: Generated 'state' is stored in db.oauth_states but never expires/garbage-collected (no TTL index).
Accepts arbitrary dict without Pydantic validation; could persist unwanted fields. Recommend a PreferencesUpdate model.
Redirect uses 307 by default via FastAPI RedirectResponse instead of 302; test allowed both. Acceptable but worth noting.

action_items: (Optional) Add TTL/cleanup for db.oauth_states to prevent unbounded growth.
(Optional) Replace `payload: dict` in PUT /api/preferences with a Pydantic model to whitelist fields.
(Optional) Pin RedirectResponse status_code=302 for OAuth error redirects for clarity.
critical_code_review_comments: PUT /api/preferences accepts raw dict — input validation missing.
exchange_code_for_tokens is sync but called from async path — acceptable but could be wrapped in run_in_executor for high concurrency.
OAuth state stored without TTL — minor data hygiene issue.
All MongoDB queries correctly project out _id; UUID-based ids used consistently — good.
test_credentials: Google OAuth only; fake session minted directly in Mongo per /app/memory/test_credentials.md (TEST_user_* prefix, auto-cleaned).
seed_data_creation: Fake users + sessions inserted with TEST_ prefix; cleaned up via fixtures after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all non-Google-live flows. To test /api/emails/* live, real Google OAuth tokens must be present in db.tokens. Public URL: https://real-time-tasks-2.preview.emergentagent.com. Fake session pattern documented in /app/memory/test_credentials.md works perfectly.
